items.title: How to Measure Customer Happiness (Paired Metrics)
items.description:
As silly as this sounds - almost no one does it:

If you’re trying to achieve something, the first step is having a way to measure progress.

And the more ways you measure, the more ways you can win.

So start there, then don’t stop winning.

items.channel_label: Tina Huang — vibe coding, AI education, workshops
items.title: 3 Claude Cowork Features That Will Change How You Use It
items.description:
If you've been using Claude Cowork, these are the three features that will completely change how you use it:

First - Skills. This is the one that makes Cowork feel like it was built specifically for you. Skills are essentially custom commands you can trigger to get Claude to do very specific things the way you want them done. Once you set these up they become muscle memory. You stop typing long prompts and start just telling it what you need in one word.

Second - Connectors. You can connect it to the tools you're already using - Gmail, Google Drive, Slack, Notion, Google Calendar, and Claude can actually read and work across all of them at the same time. So when you ask it to prep you for a meeting, it's not just working with what you tell it in the chat. It's pulling from your actual emails, your actual calendar, your actual documents. The context it has access to completely changes the quality of what it can do for you.

Third - Scheduled Tasks. You can tell Cowork to do something at a specific time - run a daily digest every morning, pull a report every Friday, follow up on something next week. You set it once, and it runs without you having to think about it again. This is the closest thing to having an assistant that works while you sleep.

The combination of all three is what makes Cowork feel different from just chatting with AI. It's less of a tool you use and more of a system that works around you.

Which one are you trying first? Drop it in the comments 👇

items.channel_label: Vaibhav Sisinty — AI-first business, growth, LinkedIn
items.title: China Just Dropped A Free AI GLM 5.2 That Beats Claude Fable 5 (+16 AI Updates)
items.description:
📲 Join our WhatsApp community for the full step-by-step guide to using GLM-5.2 for free: https://links.stayingahead.com/YT48 

GLM-5.2, a brand new free AI from China, just shook up the entire industry. In blind tests it ranked the second best model in the world, beating paid models like GPT-5.5 and nearly matching Claude, and unlike those, you can download it, run it on your own laptop, and nobody can ever ban it or shut it off. That alone would be a huge week, but it's just the first of 16 AI updates we're covering.

Anthropic, the company behind Claude, just got sued, because a customer says his $200 a month plan ran out far faster than what was promised, and the case could become a class action. An AI image company has quietly started building a full body scanner that maps you in seconds, and the reason why is the strangest part. Plus Google's $2.7 billion AI genius just left for OpenAI, NVIDIA built an AI that animates game characters and real robots, PhysicsWallah gave its AI tutor a Hindi-English voice for 36 million students, and a lot more.

We break down every major AI update this week in plain English, no jargon, made for everyone, not just developers. And right at the very end, we put GLM-5.2 head to head against Claude Fable 5, the exact model the US banned last week, and the result genuinely surprised us.

⏱️ TIMESTAMPS
01:21 - GLM-5.2: China's Free AI That Beats Claude
02:27 - Anthropic Sued Over Claude's $200 Limits
03:15 - Midjourney Is Building A Body Scanner
04:18 - Noam Shazeer Leaves Google For OpenAI
05:28 - ChatGPT Camera Feature (iOS)
06:03 - PhysicsWallah AI Voice Tutor (ElevenLabs)
06:51 - NVIDIA MotionBricks AI Animation
07:36 - OpenRouter Fusion
08:26 - ChatGPT Scheduled Tasks
08:57 - Perplexity Brain Memory
09:35 - Genspark AgentBase
10:25 - Lovable Preview Toolbar
11:12 - Claude Design Update
11:46 - Claude + Replit Integration
12:18 - Artifacts In Claude Code
13:07 - OpenAI Codex Record & Replay
13:49 - GLM-5.2 vs Claude Fable 5 vs Opus 4.8 (Full Test)

🎥 WATCH NEXT
→ Claude Fable 5 Breakdown: https://youtu.be/iH5vjVacPG8?si=hzESAxz-kec6YmQv
→ Last Week's AI Updates: https://youtu.be/ACjwmGeDHFc

This week's AI news covers GLM-5.2, the free Chinese AI that beats Claude Fable 5 and GPT-5.5, the Anthropic lawsuit over Claude's limits, Midjourney's body scanner, Noam Shazeer joining OpenAI, ChatGPT, Perplexity, NVIDIA, Replit, Lovable, Genspark, and more.
